title: Commodity Prices: The Pressure Is On
section_path: Commodity Prices: The Pressure Is On
section_path_text: Commodity Prices: The Pressure Is On
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
China is starting to see surging commodity prices as a potential threat to industrial profit margins and the continued strength of the country's industrial expansion, which by now is well beyond what can be deemed a recovery. In many aspects, the country is at risk of being a victim of its own success, with its appetite driving commodity prices higher. Recent news that Chinese authorities are requesting iron ore producers, such as BHP, to explain the surging prices must be seen as an acknowledgement that the strength of the industrial commodity markets could dent the ongoing industrial success story.
